question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send both block.number and timestamp filters simultaneously so that extractSqlFromBlockFilters() generates a WHERE clause with both index and consensus_end conditions, and by choosing values that exclude all recent blocks, cause block-time monitors to see only old blocks and compute a delay exceeding 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where blocks.length equals filters.limit, causing generateNextLink() to produce a next link anchored at last(blocks).index, and then use this link to keep a monitoring system paginating through old blocks indefinitely, never reaching the latest block and simulating a 500%+ block delay?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: validateHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a path parameter that is exactly 64 hex characters (valid eth hash) but is a known prefix of an older block's hash, causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to return that older block via the LIKE hash% query, making the apparent block time appear delayed by 500%+ of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent GET /api/v1/blocks requests with expensive filter combinations (e.g., multiple timestamp ranges) to saturate the database connection pool, causing legitimate block-time queries to queue up and return stale data, making the apparent block delay exceed 500% of the 24-hour average?
